market: AVGO remains the highest-signal filing on the watchlist: the 10-Q showed $19.3B revenue, $7.35B net income, roughly $45B of remaining performance obligations, and continued buybacks, while ONDS was the most strategically interesting small-cap filing because the BIRD acquisition package made its airborne defense footprint more concrete.
tech: This rebuild is document-led rather than template-led: six filings were read from primary text, ONDS also used attached official materials, and lower-signal governance/deal filings were clearly demoted into secondary notes instead of being padded into fake full analysis.
risk: Main caveats: ONDS still needs proof of revenue/margin contribution, BBAI's balance-sheet improvement did not fix a weak 2025 income statement, RDW still shows execution messiness, and some older watchlist filings were intentionally omitted from deep analysis rather than overclaimed.
